data.title: Fiera Capital gives Q1 results
data.description:
• Fiera Capital press release (OTCPK:FRRPF): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.17.
  • Revenue of $162.87M (-3.1% Y/Y).

data.title: N2OFF via Solterra expands European footprint with entry into fourth project; shares rise
data.description:
• N2OFF (NASDAQ:NITO) Friday announced Solterra’s entry into the Polish renewable energy market by participating in the financing of a Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) project in Poland.
  • The project, currently planned at 35MW/140MWh, represents a significant step in expanding Solterra’s large-scale energy storage solutions in the region, the company said.
  • N2OFF, along with other lenders, will assist with financing an initial payment under a signed grid connection agreement.
  • Future development plans include applying for an increase in capacity to more than 100MW/400MWh.
  • N2OFF (NASDAQ:NITO) shares up 36% to $0.52 in premarket trading.

data.title: Nippon Steel reports FY results
data.description:
• Nippon Steel Corporation (OTCPK:NPSCY): FY GAAP EPS of ¥335.15.
  • Revenue of $8.7T (-1.9% Y/Y).

data.title: Italy's Moltiply sues Google in €2.97B lawsuit over alleged market dominance
data.description:
Update: The story was updated with a statement from Google.

Italy's Moltiply Group said its unit was suing Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOGL) (NASDAQ:GOOG) Google and is seeking damages of €2.97B (about $3.34B) for alleged abuse of its dominant market position as previously recognized by the EU's Court of Justice.

Moltiply said on Friday that its subsidiary 7Pixel, which operates the price comparison service Trovaprezzi.it, recently notified several "Google Group entities of a follow-on claim for damages for the abuse of dominance that favored Google Shopping between 2010 and 2017, as finally determined by the European Court of Justice in September 2024."

Moltiply noted that the amount of damages estimated by the experts commissioned by 7Pixel, including structural effects of the abuse and interest, is equal to €2.97B.

Google noted that it is reviewing the claim.

"The changes Google made in 2017 following the European Commission’s decision are working as intended and the number of comparison shopping sites in Europe using our shopping features has multiplied from just 7 to more than 1,550. We disagree strongly with these exorbitant private damages claims which disregard this successful and growing industry," said a Google spokesperson in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha.

The claim was filed at a Milan court, Reuters reported, citing Italian daily Corriere della Sera, which did not give further details.

Last year, in September, the EU's top court upheld a fine of €2.42B imposed on Google for abusing its dominant position by favoring its comparison shopping service, Google Shopping, in search results. In 2017, the Commission accused Google of favoring Google Shopping, thereby disadvantaging competitors.

data.title: TGS Nopec Geophysical reports Q1 results
data.description:
• TGS Nopec Geophysical press release (OTCQX:TGSNF): Q1 Strong multi-client performance driven by high interest in frontier areas
  • Significant year-over-year improvement in asset utilization • Total revenues of USD 451 million compared to USD 433 million in Q1 2024
  • EBITDA of USD 258 million compared to USD 239 million³ in Q1 2024
  • Net cash flow of USD 78 million
  • Solid balance sheet allows for stable dividend of USD 0.155 per share

data.title: Vir announces 24-week post-end of treatment data for Hep B therapy
data.description:
Vir Biotechnology (NASDAQ:VIR) on Friday announced 24-week post-end-of-treatment data from a mid-stage trial for tobevibart and elebsiran, a combination therapy developed for chronic Hepatitis B in partnership with Xencor (XNCR) and Alnylam Pharma (ALNY).

The ongoing MARCH trial is designed to evaluate treatment regimens containing Xencor-partnered monoclonal antibody tobevibart and ALNY-partnered RNA therapy elebsiran (VIR-2218), alone or in combination with pegylated interferon alfa in patients with chronic hepatitis B.

According to the data related to its primary endpoint, 17% and 21% of those receiving tobevibart and elebsiran without or with PEG-IFNα indicated undetectable hepatitis B surface antigen (HBsAg), respectively.

The analysis included participants who indicated HBsAg loss (seroclearance) after 48 weeks of treatment. Those who reached 24 weeks from the end of treatment and showed less than 1,000 IU/mL of baseline HBsAg were selected for the readout.

The company has yet to strike a partnership to advance the drug combination into Phase 3 development.

data.title: Recruit Holdings reports FY results
data.description:
• Recruit Holdings press release (OTCPK:RCRRF): Q4 GAAP EPS of ¥268.30.
  • Revenue of ¥3.56B (+4.1% Y/Y).

data.title: WisdomTree reports record month-end AUM of over $116B
data.description:
• WisdomTree (NYSE:WT) reports record month-end AUM of over $116 billion.
  • Nearly $3.2 billion of net inflows year-to-date, a ~9% pace of annualized organic growth.
  • April net inflows include $650 million into U.S. and International Developed Equity product suites, a ~15% pace of annualized organic growth.

data.title: CoStar to acquire 100% of Domain Holdings
data.description:
• CoStar (NASDAQ:CSGP) said it has entered into an agreement to acquire 1Domain Holdings Australia.
  • The firm said, in February, it had acquired ~17% of the ordinary shares of Domain at A$4.20 per share for a total purchase price of ~A$452 million.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, the company has agreed to pay Domain shareholders a cash consideration of A$4.43 per share
  • The consideration to be offered to Domain shareholders values Domain at an implied enterprise value of A$3 billion.
  • The firm, thus, expects to spend ~A$2.3 billion to acquire the remaining ~83% of the ordinary shares of Domain that it doesn’t otherwise own upon consummation of the scheme.

data.title: Koppers Holdings reports mixed Q1 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Koppers Holdings press release (NYSE:KOP): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.71 beats by $0.15.
  • Revenue of $456.5M (-8.3% Y/Y) misses by $31.5M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $55.5 Million vs. $51.5 Million in Prior Year Quarter.
  • Capital expenditures of $14.3 Million vs. $26.3 Million in Prior Year Quarter.
  • Capital expenditures, net of insurance proceeds and sale of assets, of $10.0 Million vs. $25.8 Million in Prior Year Quarter.

  • Koppers is maintaining its 2025 financial goals for adjusted EBITDA of approximately $280 million, adjusted EPS of $4.75 per share and operating cash flow of $150 million.
